name: Cherry Tomato
description: Cherry tomatoes are small, round tomatoes prized for their sweet, intense flavour and prolific production. They grow on indeterminate vines and continue fruiting until frost, making them one of the most productive garden vegetables. Excellent for fresh eating, salads, and roasting.

name: Basil
description: Sweet basil is an aromatic annual herb essential to Mediterranean and Southeast Asian cuisines. It grows quickly in warm conditions and produces abundant fragrant leaves. Regular harvesting delays flowering and extends the productive season. Highly sensitive to cold.

name: Garlic
description: Garlic is a hardy bulbous plant grown for its pungent, flavourful cloves. Planted in autumn, it overwinters and is harvested in early summer. Hardneck varieties are better for cold climates; softneck varieties store longer. One of the oldest cultivated plants in human history.

name: Spinach
description: Spinach is a fast-growing cool-season leafy green rich in iron, vitamins K and A, and folate. It bolts quickly in heat, making it best suited for spring and autumn production. Baby spinach leaves can be harvested as early as 25 days after sowing.

name: Zucchini
description: Zucchini (courgette) is an extremely productive summer squash that can overwhelm a garden with its prolific output. Direct-sown after frost, it grows rapidly and produces fruit within 50–60 days. Both the fruit and edible flowers are valued in cooking.

name: Kale
description: Kale is a hardy leafy brassica that can survive hard frosts, with cold exposure actually improving its flavour by converting starches to sugars. It is among the most nutrient-dense vegetables, rich in vitamins C, K, and A. Lacinato (dinosaur) and curly kale are the most common varieties.

name: Carrot
description: Carrots are a biennial root vegetable grown as an annual. They prefer loose, deep, stone-free soil for straight root development. Poor soil leads to forked or stunted roots. Carrots are among the most widely consumed vegetables worldwide and store well under cool conditions.

name: Cucumber
description: Cucumbers are warm-season vining crops that thrive in heat and humidity. They are heavy feeders and drinkers, requiring consistent moisture to prevent bitter fruit. Harvesting frequently encourages continued production. Slicing, pickling, and English varieties have different characteristics and uses.

name: Lettuce
description: Lettuce is the world's most popular salad green, with hundreds of varieties ranging from loose-leaf to heading types. It is a cool-season crop that bolts and becomes bitter in summer heat. Successive sowings every 2–3 weeks provide continuous harvests through spring and autumn.

name: Bush Bean
description: Bush beans are compact, self-supporting annual legumes that fix atmospheric nitrogen in the soil through root nodules. They mature quickly (50–60 days) and require no staking. Green, yellow (wax), and purple varieties are available. An excellent first crop for beginner gardeners.

name: Sweet Pepper
description: Sweet peppers (capsicums) are warm-season crops from Central America that require a long growing season. They progress from green (unripe) to red, yellow, or orange (fully ripe) with increasing sweetness and vitamin C content. They need warmth, consistent moisture, and typically benefit from stake support.

name: Peas
description: Garden peas are cool-season legumes that fix nitrogen in the soil and are best sown as soon as soil can be worked in spring. They prefer cool weather; heat causes the vines to decline and pods to toughen. Varieties include shelling peas, snap peas, and snow peas. Very high in protein for a vegetable.

name: Radish
description: Radishes are one of the fastest-maturing vegetables, with small varieties ready to harvest in 20–30 days from sowing. A cool-season crop that bolts in heat. Often grown as a catch crop or to mark rows of slower-germinating seeds. Daikon radishes are large storage varieties from Asian cuisines.

name: Sunflower
description: Sunflowers are tall, fast-growing annuals grown for their edible seeds, cooking oil, and as ornamentals. They grow best in full sun with well-drained soil and tolerate drought once established. Giant varieties can reach 3 m tall. Young flower buds are edible and the petals are used as food garnishes.

name: Sweet Potato
description: Sweet potatoes are tender perennials grown as warm-season annuals, producing nutritious, sweet-fleshed tubers in tropical to temperate climates. They require a long frost-free season (90–120 days) and thrive in sandy, well-drained soil. The leaves and tips are also edible as greens.

name: Beet
description: Beets (beetroot) are cool-season root vegetables grown for both their earthy, sweet roots and their nutritious greens. A single seed cluster contains 2–4 seeds, requiring thinning. Both the roots and greens are edible. Beets store well in cool, moist conditions for several months.

name: Thyme
description: Common thyme is a hardy perennial Mediterranean herb with small, aromatic leaves used fresh or dried in cooking. It is drought-tolerant once established and grows well in poor, well-drained soils. An excellent companion plant that repels pests and attracts pollinators.

name: Mint
description: Spearmint is a vigorous, spreading perennial herb best grown in containers to prevent it from becoming invasive. It spreads aggressively by underground runners (stolons) and can take over a garden bed within a single season. Leaves are used fresh or dried for teas, cooking, and garnish.

name: Broccoli
description: Broccoli is a cool-season brassica grown for its dense green flower heads. It requires consistent moisture and does not tolerate heat well; heads should be harvested before flowers open. After the main head is cut, side shoots continue to produce smaller florets for weeks.

name: Onion
description: Onions are biennial bulbing plants grown as annuals. They are day-length sensitive — short-day varieties suit southern regions, long-day varieties suit northern regions. Growing from sets (small bulbs) is faster and easier than from seed. Essential in most of the world's cuisines.

name: Parsley
description: Parsley is a biennial herb grown as an annual, used as a garnish and flavouring in European and Middle Eastern cuisines. Curly parsley is more decorative; Italian flat-leaf parsley is more flavourful. It is slow to germinate (3–4 weeks) and benefits from pre-soaking seed.

name: Strawberry
description: Garden strawberries are perennial fruiting plants grown for their sweet red berries. June-bearing varieties produce a single large crop; everbearing varieties produce smaller crops across the season. Plants spread by runners (stolons), which can be rooted to establish new plants. Rich in vitamin C.

name: Pumpkin
description: Pumpkins are large, sprawling warm-season vining crops that require substantial space. They are grown for their large, sweet fruits used in cooking, baking, and decoration. Each plant requires 2–4 m of space and benefits from rich, well-amended soil. Cures at room temperature extend storage life.

name: Dill
description: Dill is a fast-growing annual herb with feathery leaves and aromatic seeds, both of which are used in cooking. It bolts quickly in heat, producing flowers and seeds. Dill self-seeds readily and can naturalise in a garden. Do not plant near fennel — they cross-pollinate and reduce seed quality of both.

name: Chive
description: Chives are perennial alliums grown for their mild, onion-flavoured hollow leaves. Among the hardiest herbs, they die back in winter and return vigorously in spring. Both the leaves and purple flowers are edible. Cut to 2–3 cm above soil to encourage fresh growth throughout the season.

name: Eggplant
description: Eggplant (aubergine) is a heat-loving member of the nightshade family that requires a long, warm growing season. It is slow to establish but productive once it begins fruiting. Asian varieties are slender and mild; Italian varieties are large and meaty. Requires staking when fruit develops.

name: Swiss Chard
description: Swiss chard is a leafy green related to beets, grown for its large, tender leaves and colourful, crunchy stems. More heat-tolerant than spinach and more cold-tolerant than basil, it is one of the most reliable greens for a long growing season. Coloured varieties (Rainbow chard) are highly ornamental.

name: Watermelon
description: Watermelons are large, sprawling warm-season crops requiring substantial space, full sun, and a long frost-free growing season. They thrive in hot, dry climates with consistent deep watering. Sugar content and ripeness are judged by tendril browning near the fruit, ground spot colour, and hollow sound when tapped.

name: Rosemary
description: Rosemary is a woody perennial Mediterranean herb with needle-like, intensely aromatic leaves. It thrives in poor, well-drained soil and drought conditions; overwatering is the most common cause of death. In cold climates it is grown as an annual or brought indoors in winter. Long-lived (20+ years) in suitable climates.

name: Corn
description: Sweet corn is a warm-season annual grass grown for its sweet, starchy kernels. It is wind-pollinated and must be planted in blocks (not rows) for good pollination. Sugars convert rapidly to starch after harvest, so corn is best eaten immediately or preserved quickly. Requires fertile, consistently moist soil.

name: Cilantro
description: Cilantro (coriander) is a fast-growing annual herb widely used in Latin American, South Asian, and Southeast Asian cuisines. It bolts rapidly in heat, producing seeds (coriander) which are a separate spice. Successive sowings every 2–3 weeks extend the leaf harvest. Some people have a genetic predisposition to perceive cilantro as soapy.

name: Potato
description: Potatoes are the world's most widely grown vegetable, cultivated for their starchy tubers. They grow from seed potatoes (tuber pieces with eyes) rather than true seed. Hilling soil up around the plant as it grows increases tuber yield and prevents greening from sun exposure. Harvested after tops die back.
